cot_prefix: Okay, let's read the passage again and find out why Babbage failed to continue his research in 1828. The question is asking for the main reason Babbage couldn't continue his research in 1828. The options are A) he was in poor health, B) he ran out of money, C) co-workers argued with him, or D) spent all money on bills. Looking at the passage: When he returned to London in 1828, he was at the end of his resources. Many bills remained unpaid. His chief assistant and co-workers quarreled with him and left with many expensive tools. For one year no work was done. The key sentence here is "he was at the end of his resources." That suggests he had no money left. His co-workers left, but that seems to be a result of the situation rather than the main cause. The fact that he had to take a long holiday abroad because of health issues was mentioned before 1828 ("Then his health broke down..."). So his poor health
